title: Getting Started with dafr
text: Daf (Data Axes Format) multi-axis container biological / scientific data. Think typed dictionary scalars, per-axis vectors, per-axis-pair matrices, cache invalidation lazy mmap-backed reads.

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: FilesDaf directory-based storage format Daf data model. directory tree holds scalars, axes, vectors, matrices individual files, self-describing, trivially machine-readable form. every property lives file, standard OS tools (ls, wc, od, make) can inspect manipulate data without special library. trade-store directory rather single archive; shipping requires zip, tar, similar. binary data stored contiguous sequence fixed-size elements little-endian byte order headers alignment padding (files_format.jl:111–113). String data stored one entry per line, terminated \n regardless host OS (files_format.jl:115–117). Dense matrices stored column-major (Fortran / Julia-native) layout (files_format.jl:57–58, files_format.jl:111–113).

title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: valid FilesDaf root directory contains exactly following structure (files_format.jl:18–19, files_format.jl:222–226): four subdirectories created unconditionally store initialised (files_format.jl:222–226). directory lacks daf.json rejected “daf directory” (files_format.jl:230–231). Unknown files subdirectories root subdirectory silently ignored reader; discovery suffix (.json scalars property descriptors, .txt axes), unrecognised files cause error (files_format.jl:959–972).
code: <root>/ ├── daf.json                    # required — format sentinel and version ├── scalars/                    # required — scalar properties ├── axes/                       # required — axis entry lists ├── vectors/                    # required — per-axis vector properties └── matrices/                   # required — per-axis-pair matrix properties

Compatibility rule (files_format.jl:239–245): reader must reject store whose major version differs one knows (1), must reject store whose minor version greater maximum supports (0). reader may open stores lower minor version maximum. title: FilesDaf on-disk format
text: FilesDaf format provides atomic-write crash-recovery guarantees filesystem level. Writes performed : Opening target file open(path, "w") writing content (files_format.jl:280, files_format.jl:339, files_format.jl:447–449). writing entire files via write(path, data) binary arrays (files_format.jl:436–439, files_format.jl:671–675). use .tmp sibling files atomic rename (mv). explicit fsync call. crash mid-write may leave partially-written file. Concurrent-writer safety handled entirely -process via Julia read / write locks (Formats.has_data_write_lock), filesystem-level mechanisms. Multi-process concurrent writes FilesDaf store safe. Single-writer contract. FilesDaf format provides filesystem-level atomicity. writer MUST complete every file write associated property (descriptor + payload) reader opens store, one writer may touch given store time. Multi-process multi-threaded concurrent writers require external coordination (e.g., filesystem lock root directory). Readers MAY observe partially-written store crash mid-write; recovery restoration backup, -format rollback. External writers (outside reference implementations) MUST follow rule: property considered “written” files — descriptor JSON plus binary payload(s) — present disk.

title: Query DSL
text: equivalent string form uses >- (reduce--row) >| (reduce--column):
code: # Per-gene mean UMI — reduce across the cell axis per_gene_mean <- d[Axis("gene") |> Axis("cell") |> LookupMatrix("UMIs") |> ReduceToRow(Mean())] head(per_gene_mean) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                            3.351391                            4.535871  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                            2.411420                            4.131772  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                            2.641288                            3.929722 head(d["@ gene @ cell :: UMIs >- Mean"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                            3.351391                            4.535871  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                            2.411420                            4.131772  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                            2.641288                            3.929722

title: Query DSL
code: # Donors older than 30 (the `age` vector lives on the `donor` axis) q <- Axis("donor") |> BeginMask("age") |> IsGreater(30) |> EndMask() length(d[q]) #> [1] 93

title: Query DSL reference
text: query sequence operators executed one operator time. Instead, parser groups operators phrases, phrase stack-rewriting step: query state stack, starting empty. phrase matches pattern top stack (example, LookupVector phrase needs axis specification top). phrase pops matching top elements, performs operation, pushes zero new elements. operators consumed, stack must hold exactly one result; result query returns. grouping--phrases means operator’s meaning depends phrase participates . table operators given , phrase column indicates phrase operator appears .

title: Query DSL reference
text: Element-wise operation builders include Abs, Clamp, Convert, Fraction, Log, Round, Significant. Reduction operation builders include Sum, Mean, Median, Mode, Quantile, Max, Min, Count, GeoMean, Std, StdN, Var, VarN.

title: Query DSL reference
text: Every query returns one four result kinds, selected phrases uses.

title: Query DSL reference
text: Returns set names point data hierarchy.
code: cells[". ?"] #> [1] "organism"  "reference" cells["@ gene : ?"] #> [1] "is_lateral" cells["@ cell @ gene :: ?"] #> [1] "UMIs"

title: Query DSL reference
text: Returns single scalar value. Produced : Looking scalar (. name), optionally || default optional type T pin default’s dtype. Looking vector picking one entry (: vec @ axis = entry). Looking matrix picking one cell (:: m @ rows = R @ cols = C). Reducing vector scalar (... >> Reduction). Reducing matrix scalar (... >> Reduction).
code: cells[". organism"] #> [1] "human" cells[". nope || fallback"] #> [1] "fallback" cells[". nope || 0 type Int64"] #> integer64 #> [1] 0  # Pick one entry from a vector. cells["@ donor : age @ donor = N89"] #> [1] 55  # Number of genes marked as lateral (reduce a vector of Bool). cells["@ gene : is_lateral >> Sum type Int64"] #> [1] 438  # Total UMIs in the whole matrix. cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs >> Sum type Int64"] #> [1] 1171936

title: Query DSL reference
text: Returns vector. Phrase kinds: Axis entries: @ axis. Axis entries masking: @ axis [ ... ]. Property lookup along axis: @ axis : property. element-wise op appended: @ axis : property % Op. Reduction matrix along one dimension: @ rows @ cols :: m >- Op (row result) >| Op (column result).
code: head(cells["@ experiment"]) #> [1] "demux_01_02_21_1" "demux_01_02_21_2" "demux_01_03_21_1" "demux_04_01_21_1" #> [5] "demux_04_01_21_2" "demux_07_03_21_1" length(cells["@ gene [ ! is_lateral ]"]) #> [1] 245 head(cells["@ cell : donor"]) #> [1] "N89" "N84" "N86" "N84" "N89" "N89" # Per-row (per-gene) mean UMIs. head(cells["@ gene @ cell :: UMIs >- Mean"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                            3.351391                            4.535871  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                            2.411420                            4.131772  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                            2.641288                            3.929722

title: Query DSL reference
text: Returns matrix. Phrase kinds: Lookup: @ rows @ cols :: property. Count value-combinations: @ axis : v1 * v2. followed element-wise op, row / column grouping + reduction.
code: dim(cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs"]) #> [1] 856 683  # Log-transform a dense matrix. dim(cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs % Log base 2 eps 1"]) #> [1] 856 683  # Group matrix rows by donor, then reduce each group to its mean. dim(cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs -/ donor >- Mean"]) #> [1]  95 683

title: Query DSL reference
text: mask filters axis entries predicate holds. Phrase structure: @ axis [ <mask-body> ]. mask body starts property lookup (BeginMask) followed zero comparisons mask combinators. Supported comparisons: <, <=, =, !=, >=, >, ~ (regex match), !~ (regex non-match). Mask combinators: &, & !, |, | !, ^, ^ !. evaluated left right — grouped precedence. Use separate masks explicit combinators order matters.
code: # Donors older than 30. length(cells[Axis("donor") |>              BeginMask("age") |> IsGreater(30) |> EndMask()]) #> [1] 93  # Same in string form. length(cells["@ donor [ age > 30 ]"]) #> [1] 93  # Negated mask: genes that are NOT lateral. length(cells["@ gene [ ! is_lateral ]"]) #> [1] 245  # Combined masks: age > 60 AND sex = male (on donor axis). length(cells["@ donor [ age > 60 & sex = male ]"]) #> [1] 29

title: Query DSL reference
text: Two operators supply fallback values: || value (IfMissing) — used thing looked exist, reducing empty vector / matrix. ?? value (IfNot) — used chained lookups (: =@ : b) intermediate key empty.
code: cells[". nope || fallback"] #> [1] "fallback"  # Fallback when a chained lookup runs out of pivot values. head(metacells["@ cell : metacell =@ ?? none : type"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                              "none"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                               "MPP"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                              "none"

title: Query DSL reference
text: Element-wise ops applied %; reductions matrix >- (reduce row) >| (reduce column). take keyword-style sub-arguments separated spaces: Built-element-wise: Abs, Clamp low h, Convert type T, Fraction, Log base b eps e, Round, Significant digits n. Built-reductions: Sum, Mean, Median, Mode, Quantile p P, Max, Min, Count, GeoMean, Std, StdN, Var, VarN. User code can extend either set:
code: daf["... % Log base 2 eps 1e-5"] daf["... >- Mean"] daf["... >| Sum type Int64"] register_eltwise("MySquare", function(x) x * x) register_reduction("MyMax", function(x) max(x, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Query DSL reference
text: Grouping appears three phrase contexts: GroupBy (/) — reduce vector groups defined another vector equal length, yielding shorter vector keyed group. GroupRowsBy (-/) — group rows matrix emit one row per group. GroupColumnsBy (|/) — group columns matrix emit one column per group. matrix-group forms must paired compatible reduction (-/ pairs >-, |/ >|). Appending =@ group property changes result ordered axis’s entries (rather unique group value). Appending || default reduction supplies value empty groups.
code: # Mean per (donor, gene) — collapse cells into donor rows. dim(cells["@ cell @ gene :: UMIs -/ donor >- Mean"]) #> [1]  95 683

title: Query DSL reference
text: =@ declares set values interpreted entries named axis. lets query walk one axis another: , : metacell =@ says “values entries metacell axis”, : type looks type property axis. ?? NA fallback covers cells without assigned metacell. SquareColumnIs (@|) SquareRowIs (@-) slice square matrix specific column / row axis; right-hand side value matrix’s square axis.
code: # Cell -> metacell -> type: pipe values through the metacell axis. head(metacells["@ cell : metacell =@ ?? none : type"]) #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                              "none"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1 demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                               "MPP"  #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1  #>                          "MEBEMP-E"                              "none"

title: Query DSL reference
text: parse_query(s) returns builder AST; canonical_query(q) returns normalised string; forms query produce AST. Inspection helpers: has_query(daf, q) — TRUE q evaluated daf. is_axis_query(q) — TRUE result axis. query_axis_name(q) — axis name (applicable). query_result_dimensions(q) — 0 (scalar), 1 (vector), 2 (matrix). query_requires_relayout(q) — whether matrix relayout needed.
code: s     <- "@ gene : is_lateral" q_bld <- Axis("gene") |> LookupVector("is_lateral") identical(canonical_query(s), as.character(q_bld)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Query DSL reference
text: String literal values inside query can include spaces punctuation. Characters collide DSL’s tokens (@, :, |, [, ], …) must escaped. Use escape_value() produce safe literal unescape_value() reverse .

title: Query DSL reference
text: get_query(daf, q) caches result QueryData inside daf’s internal cache. Use daf[q] one-shot evaluation cache. empty_cache(daf) releases cached results also invoked automatically every mutating write daf cached results never become stale.

text: dafr native R + C++ implementation DataAxesFormats (DAF) data model: uniform, self-describing container 1D 2D data arranged along set named axes. Think generalisation AnnData scalars, per-axis vectors, per-axis-pair matrices, disk-backed persistence, composable query DSL. package pure R + C++ port Julia reference implementation — Julia install required.

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: Julia install required — pure R + C++ (JuliaCall copy tax). Mmap-backed reads via mmap_dgCMatrix / mmap_int / mmap_lgl / mmap_real. OpenMP-parallel kernels Sum / Mean / Var / Mode / Quantile / GeoMean. register_eltwise / register_reduction user-defined query ops. Pipe-chain builders: daf[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("age") |> IsGreater(2)]. AnnData interop via DafAnnData + h5ad_as_daf / daf_as_h5ad.

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
code: library(dafr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dafr' #> The following object is masked from 'package:graphics': #>  #>     Axis  # Create a DAF object in memory daf <- memory_daf("example")  # Add axes add_axis(daf, "obs", c("cell1", "cell2", "cell3")) add_axis(daf, "var", c("gene1", "gene2"))  # Add vector data set_vector(daf, "obs", "score", c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9))  # Add matrix data mat <- matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) set_matrix(daf, "obs", "var", "counts", mat)  # Access data get_vector(daf, "obs", "score") #> cell1 cell2 cell3  #>   0.1   0.5   0.9 get_matrix(daf, "obs", "var", "counts") #>       gene1 gene2 #> cell1     1     4 #> cell2     2     5 #> cell3     3     6  # Get a dataframe view get_dataframe(daf, "obs") #>       score #> cell1   0.1 #> cell2   0.5 #> cell3   0.9

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: dafr provides two equivalent query interfaces: compact string DSL pipe-composable builders. [ operator DafReader accepts either form. query syntax follows simple pattern: @ axis selects axis . property retrieves scalar : property retrieves vector property :: property retrieves matrix property % operation applies element-wise operation >> reduction applies reduction operation [ property comparison value ] filters property See upstream Julia documentation full query language reference — native parser semantically equivalent.
code: # Create a sample dataset daf <- memory_daf("query_examples") add_axis(daf, "cell", c("A", "B", "C")) add_axis(daf, "gene", c("X", "Y", "Z")) set_vector(daf, "cell", "age", c(10, 50, 70)) set_vector(daf, "cell", "type", c("T", "B", "NK")) set_scalar(daf, "version", "1.0") set_matrix(daf, "cell", "gene", "counts", matrix(1:9, nrow = 3, ncol = 3))  # Scalar daf[". version"] #> [1] "1.0"  # Axes daf["@ ?"] #> [1] "cell" "gene" daf["@ cell : ?"] #> [1] "age"  "type"  # Vector data daf["@ cell : age"] #> [1] 10 50 70  # Matrix data daf["@ cell @ gene :: counts"] #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    1    4    7 #> [2,]    2    5    8 #> [3,]    3    6    9  # Filter axis by property daf["@ cell [ age > 20 ]"] #> [1] "B" "C"  # Transform with eltwise op daf["@ cell : age % Abs"] #> [1] 10 50 70  # Pipe-chain builder form daf[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("age")] #> [1] 10 50 70  # Composed builder: mask + vector lookup daf[     Axis("cell") |>         BeginMask("age") |>         IsGreater(20) |>         EndMask() ] #> [1] "B" "C"

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: Daf object can exposed AnnData-shaped facade familiar X / obs / var / layers / uns / obs_names / var_names / shape properties: facade read-; writes (ann$X <- ...) raise error. Modify underlying Daf directly need change data. Round-trip h5ad /O supported (requires hdf5r Suggests dep). Sparse X, categorical obs / var columns, nested uns, obsm / varm round-trip:
code: d <- example_cells_daf() ann <- as_anndata(d)  ann$n_obs #> [1] 856 ann$n_vars #> [1] 683 dim(ann$X) #> [1] 856 683 head(ann$obs_names) #> [1] "demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1" "demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1" #> [3] "demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1" "demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1" #> [5] "demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1" "demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1" head(ann$obs) #>                                     donor       experiment #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_AACGAAAGTCCAATCA-1   N84 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_AAGACAAAGTTCCGTA-1   N86 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGACTCATCTATTGTC-1   N84 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 d  <- h5ad_as_daf("path/to/file.h5ad") daf_as_h5ad(d, "out.h5ad", overwrite = TRUE)

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: tbl(daf, axis) returns lazy daf_axis_tbl rows axis entries columns per-axis vectors. dplyr verbs work natively: Supported verbs: filter, select, mutate, arrange, summarise, group_by / ungroup, distinct, pull, collect, as_tibble, count / tally / add_count / add_tally. grouping variable names another axis already daf, summarise() count() auto-tie-back lazy daf_axis_tbl axis — pipeline can continue. Axis entries appear name column (convention axis-identity column). Example: Mutations accumulated lazily can written back dplyr::compute(tbl, vectors = c(...)): Matrices scope — use native query DSL .
code: library(dplyr)  d <- example_cells_daf()  tbl(d, "cell") |>     filter(donor == "N89") |>     select(name, donor, experiment) |>     collect() |>     head() #> # A tibble: 6 x 3 #>                                  name donor       experiment #> 1 demux_07_12_20_1_AACAAGATCCATTTCA-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 2 demux_07_12_20_1_AGATAGACATTCCTCG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 3 demux_07_12_20_1_ATCGTAGTCCAGTGCG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 4 demux_07_12_20_1_CACAGGCGTCCTACAA-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 5 demux_07_12_20_1_CCTACGTAGCCAACCC-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1 #> 6 demux_07_12_20_1_GAAGGGTGTCCCTGAG-1   N89 demux_07_12_20_1  tbl(d, "cell") |>     count(experiment, sort = TRUE) |>     collect() |>     head() #> # A tibble: 6 x 2 #>               name  n #> 1 demux_28_12_20_2 72 #> 2 demux_28_12_20_1 58 #> 3 demux_21_02_21_2 51 #> 4 demux_22_02_21_2 47 #> 5 demux_01_03_21_1 45 #> 6 demux_07_12_20_2 42 # `donor` is an axis of example_cells_daf(); count() re-anchors onto it. tbl(d, "cell") |>     count(donor, sort = TRUE) |>     class()   # daf_axis_tbl + tbl_df #> [1] "daf_axis_tbl" # Lazy pipeline... updated <- tbl(d, "cell") |>     mutate(donor_upper = toupper(donor))  # ...materialise the new vector on the underlying daf: dplyr::compute(updated, vectors = "donor_upper")

title: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R
text: Full Daf data model: scalars, per-axis vectors, per-axis-pair matrices. -memory (memory_daf) -disk (files_daf) stores. Zero-copy mmap-backed reads vectors sparse matrices read-FilesDaf. OpenMP-parallel reduction kernels (Sum, Mean, Var, Mode, Quantile, GeoMean). Views adapters zero-copy subsetting / renaming. Chaining repositories efficient overlay / reuse data. Contracts explicit computation input / output validation. Explicit support grouped axes group helpers. Query DSL (string form + pipe-chain builders) user-extensible ops (register_eltwise, register_reduction). AnnData (h5ad) import / export. Control data layout support dense sparse matrices.

code: adapter(   daf,   fn,   input_axes = NULL,   input_data = NULL,   output_axes = NULL,   output_data = NULL,   capture = memory_daf,   empty = NULL,   relayout = TRUE,   overwrite = FALSE,   name = ".adapter" )

title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
text: daf DafWriter — base data read write . fn function taking single DafWriter argument (adapted chain). Return value passes . input_axes, input_data Passed viewer() input view. least one output_axes / output_data must non-NULL (otherwise adapter() degenerates fn(daf)). output_axes, output_data Passed viewer() output view. capture Factory function returning fresh DafWriter. Default memory_daf. empty Named list list("<axis>|<vector>" = default, "<r>|<c>|<m>" = default) supplying default values entries present daf's axis absent source view's. NULL (default) disables feature. relayout TRUE (default), matrix copies also write transposed layout. overwrite TRUE, pre-existing destination entries replaced. name Human-readable name input/capture/adapted dafs. Default ".adapter".

title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
text: return value fn(adapted).

title: Apply a computation to a renaming view of a DafWriter. — adapter
text: Flow: input = viewer(daf, axes = input_axes, data = input_data) exposes subset computation consumes, possibly renamed axes / names. capture = capture_factory(name = "<base>.capture") fresh writable. adapted = chain_writer(list(input, capture)) — reads fall input, writes go capture. result = fn(adapted) — computation's return value. output = viewer(adapted, axes = output_axes, data = output_data) — selects + renames outputs. Copy output daf via internal helper. Honors overwrite, relayout, empty. Return result.

what: dafr: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R — dafr-package
title: dafr: Data in Axes in Formats (DAF) for R — dafr-package
text: native R implementation 'DataAxesFormats.jl' https://github.com/tanaylab/DataAxesFormats.jl data model, providing uniform interface accessing 1D 2D data arranged along arbitrary axes. generalizes extends 'AnnData'-like functionality, supporting scalars multiple data modalities (e.g. RNA-seq, ATAC-seq), efficient memory management via memory-mapped reads, composable query DSL. Unlike 'dafJuliaWrapper' https://github.com/tanaylab/dafJuliaWrapper interface, 'dafr' 'Julia' dependency; storage, query evaluation, reductions implemented directly R C++ optional 'OpenMP' parallelism. package ships -memory ('MemoryDaf') file-backed ('FilesDaf') storage, 'AnnData' facade h5ad read/write, computation contracts pre/post-condition validation, 'dplyr' backend tidy-verbs access axis data.

what: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
text: Mirrors fast-path % Log takes inside query evaluator, callable directly -memory numeric matrix vector without wrapping input memory_daf(). Intended downstream packages whose hot paths already matrix materialised R (e.g. session-level cache) want avoid single-threaded log(x + eps, base) query fallback otherwise incur.

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
code: fast_log(   x,   eps = 0,   base = exp(1),   threshold = dafr_opt("dafr.kernel_threshold") )

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
text: x Dense numeric matrix numeric vector. Integers silently promoted double. eps Non-negative scalar added inside log. Default 0. base Positive scalar. Default exp(1). threshold Parallel-threshold: enable OpenMP length(x) >= threshold. Defaults dafr_opt("dafr.kernel_threshold").

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
text: numeric matrix vector matching shape x. Names dimnames preserved.

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log
text: base values 2, 10, exp(1) dispatch std::log2, std::log10, std::log respectively — matching R's preference accurate form log(x) / log(base). Result equal log(x + eps, base) machine precision (verified query-julia-compat parity suite).

title: Parallel log(x + eps) / log(base) on dense numeric input. — fast_log

File-backed Daf store. — files_daf
text: path Directory path. mode One "r" (read-, store must exist), "r+" (read-write, store must exist), "w" (create; fails store already exists), "w+" (create open existing store; store already exists, scalars, axes, vectors, matrices subdirectories daf.json truncated, unrelated files directory preserved). name Human-readable identifier. title: dafr 0.1.0 (development)
text: Three DataAxesFormats.jl query-DSL features previously missing implemented, closing last semantic gaps dafr upstream Julia package: >> Reduction reduces vector matrix scalar (e.g. @ gene : is_lateral >> Sum type Int64, @ cell @ gene :: UMIs >> Sum). >> longer silently aliased >|; grouped input (... / g >> Sum) continues produce per-group vector, . @ axis = entry picks one entry vector (@ cell : age @ cell = N89) one cell matrix (@ cell @ gene :: UMIs @ cell = C @ gene = X). Two successive picks collapse matrix scalar. || value type T attaches Julia-style dtype (Bool, Int8..Int64, UInt8..UInt64, Float32, Float64, String) scalar-lookup default, matching existing behaviour suffix inside reductions element-wise ops. IfMissing() builder gains optional type kwarg (IfMissing(0, type = "Int64")).

title: dafr 0.1.0
text: First public release. native R + C++ implementation DataAxesFormats (DAF) data model multi-dimensional data along arbitrary axes, ported Julia reference implementation Julia dependency.

title: dafr 0.1.0
text: Core model. Scalars, per-axis vectors, per-axis-pair matrices, axis entries, cache invalidation. memory_daf() files_daf() backends. Query DSL. String form (daf["@ cell : donor"]) pipe-chain builders (daf[Axis("cell") |> LookupVector("donor") |> IsGreater(2)]). Memory-mapped reads vectors sparse matrices read-files_daf() store (zero-copy). OpenMP-parallel C++ kernels Sum / Mean / Var / Mode / Quantile / GeoMean, CRAN-compliant 2-core auto-cap via set_num_threads(). AnnData interop. DafAnnData R6 facade, as_anndata(), h5ad_as_daf(), daf_as_h5ad() — sparse X, categorical obs/var, nested uns, obsm/varm round-trip. dplyr backend. tbl(daf, axis) → lazy daf_axis_tbl filter, select, mutate, arrange, summarise, group_by, distinct, pull, collect, compute (write-back). Contracts. create_contract(), verify_contract(), contract_scalar() / contract_vector() / contract_matrix() / tensor_contract() / axis_contract() computation pre/post-condition validation. See pkgdown site full documentation.
